title: Const all the floating point arithmetic
author: davidgraymi
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

Floating point arithmetic can now be used in constant functions! This is a valuable API improvement, as it allows users to define their own derived constants using our handy math operations.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

We should constify our APIs, one library at a time. This should be kept to relatively simple functions and methods: only things that we can commit to being const indefinitely.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Use a quick sweep of `cargo clippy --fix --allow-dirty -- -W clippy::missing_const_for_fn` to set all functions that allow it to const.

**Additional context**

I come from [bevy](https://github.com/bevyengine/bevy) and want to support https://github.com/bevyengine/bevy/issues/16124.

title: Add .is_negative_mask() and .is_negative() for all vector types
author: VoidAndEther
body:
What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?

This solves the problem of having to manually writing out a function for .is_negative_mask() every time you need to check for the signs of the vector's elements

Describe the solution you'd like

Implement the method .is_negative_mask() for all Vector types except BVec3

Describe alternatives you've considered

vector.signum().cmpeq(Vec3::NEG_ONE)
vector.cmplt(Vec3::ZERO)

title: Quat::ZERO is private
author: BadMachine
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

access to the constant

**Describe the solution you'd like**

make it public

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Quat::from_array([0.0; 4])

title: Wincode
author: suprohub
body: What about implementing traits from [wincode](https://lib.rs/crates/wincode)?

title: Suggestion: Move the `fast-math` feature to a `--cfg` flag
author: LikeLakers2
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

The `fast-math` feature, as currently described in the readme, is not meant to be used by intermediate libraries:
> Intermediate libraries should not use this feature and defer the decision to the final binary build.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

While the above warning should be sufficient, we can do a little better to further discourage intermediate libraries from using this feature. Specifically, we can change `fast-math` from being specified as `--features glam/fast-math` to `--cfg glam_fast_math`. (I'd suggest adding a `glam_` prefix so as not to conflict with other crates that might have a "fast math" mode.)

Users would then specify this using rustflags - either through the `RUSTFLAGS` environment variable, or through the `build.rustflags` option in `.cargo/config.toml`.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Leaving it as is (which I admit may be a desirable option)

**Additional context**

This idea came to me after I saw how the `getrandom` and `sha2` crates allow specifying various backend stuff through `--cfg` flags. See <https://docs.rs/sha2/0.11.0/sha2/#backends> and <https://docs.rs/getrandom/0.4.2/getrandom/#opt-in-backends>.

If implementing this change is desired, I am willing to make a PR, as the changes are relatively simple.

title: All `map()`-Methods could take `FnMut`-Closures as arguments
author: bitshifter
body:
### Discussed in https://github.com/bitshifter/glam-rs/discussions/731
Originally posted by **StT191** May 28, 2026 Currently all the `map()`-methods of the `glam` vector-types require the mapping function to implement `Fn`. However they could all take a function with a more flexible `FnMut` requirement like the primitive `array`-types [do](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/primitive.array.html#method.map) in `std`. Of course anybody who would need to map with a `FnMut`-closure could convert to/from `array` and use the `array`-map-method. But being able to use the `map()`-methods of the `glam`-types would be much nicer in that case.

title: support `core-simd` in no-std
author: vxpm
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

currently, the `core::simd` backend is only supported if `std` is enabled. this is because of `core::simd::StdFloat`, which assumes `std` is available. it would be interesting to use `core::simd` in no-std environments.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

allow usage of `core::simd` even without `std` by falling back to scalar `libm` code.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

keeping it as is, or gating methods that depend on `StdFloat` behind `std` when `core-simd` is also enabled. 

**Additional context**

the use case is simply `no-std` with weird ISAs that don't have specific implementations in glam.

title: XZ Coords in Vec2
author: suprohub
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

Current vector is:
```rust
pub struct IVec2 {
    pub x: i32,
    pub y: i32,
}
```

Not all games are Z based. Some games are Y based, and if devs want use Vec2 in this games (for chunk system for example), they mental map will fall apart because 2 Y's meaning 2 different things.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

```rust
pub struct IVec2XZ {
    pub x: i32,
    pub z: i32,
}
```

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Name can be also IVec2Z.
Maybe somehow implement macros for auto generating xz and replacing y everywhere.

**Additional context**
My use case its minecraft custom server.

title: scalar / matrix implementation is wrong
author: bitshifter
body:
The implementation of `scalar / matrix` is backwards and is actually performing `matrix / scalar`.

It should return a matrix where every element is `scalar / element`.

title: Add support for wasm64, remove hard wasm32 dependency
author: barabadzhi
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

Today `glam` relies on `core::arch::wasm32` intrinsics heavily for `wasm` family of targets.
With introduction of `wasm64-unknown-unknown` it becomes unusable as it simply did not compile on `wasm64`.

Why we may need this? Simply, more memory to address  = more objects to draw. Nom-nom 😄 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾  

**Describe the solution you'd like**

Remove hard dependency on `core::arch::wasm32` intrinsics.
Conditional `target`-based compilation is likely a way to go.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Switchiong away from `glam` in the meantime.
Hand-rolling math primitives (not really in scope).

How do you intend to use it?

To turn this...
👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾

into THIS...
👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾
👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾 👾

title: Add missing `ISizeVec{2,3,4}` types
author: ItzShiney
body: Add the missing counterpart for `USizeVec{2,3,4}`. I believe it may be useful as the default option for "I need signed integers with the best possible range to execution time ratio". As `{i,u}size` types, I believe, are always the best in this regard. I'm actually surprised no one has requested this here yet.

title: Adopted matrix extensions from glam_matrix_extras and glamx
author: bitshifter
body:
Physics libraries Avian and Rapier both use glam and matrix extension traits for some features that glam doesn't have. Some of these look generally useful.

* https://github.com/Jondolf/glam_matrix_extras
* https://github.com/dimforge/glamx

Definitely add:
*  `fn diagonal(&self) -> Self::Vector`
* `fn inverse_or_zero(&self) -> Self`
* `fn try_inverse(&self) -> Option<Self>`

Possibly add:
* `fn is_symmetric(&self) -> bool`
* `fn from_outer_product(a: Self::Vector, b: Self::Vector) -> Self`

title: Make a pass over Embark's macaw to identify things that make sense for core glam
author: bitshifter
body:
Macaw added a bunch of extensions to glam, it would be worth going through these and seeing which things make sense to add to core glam

https://github.com/EmbarkStudios/macaw

- [x] Affine3 (no SIMD so it's smaller) - Bevy has also added this so I think it makes sense to make in glam
- [x] FloatExt - glam already has some of these, also compare with what rust-gpu adds and add common stuff
- [x] Mat3Ex - mul_diagonal_scale -  a little niche but potentially nice optimization for a common operation
- [x] QuatEx - rotate_negative_z_towards and rotate_positive_z_towards - maybe too niche
- [x] Vec2Ex
- [x] Vec3Ex
- [x] Vec3Ex

title: Can't build `glam=0.30.9`
author: JoelBeicher
body:
**Describe the bug**

`glam@=0.30.9` not compiling.

**To Reproduce**

1. `cargo new new-project`
2. `cargo add glam@=0.30.9`
3. `cd new-project`
4. `cargo build`

**Expected behavior**

To compile successfully.

**System information:**

rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08)
binary: rustc
commit-hash: ded5c06cf21d2b93bffd5d884aa6e96934ee4234
commit-date: 2025-12-08
host: aarch64-apple-darwin
release: 1.92.0
LLVM version: 21.1.3
cargo 1.92.0 (344c4567c 2025-10-21)

CPU: Apple M2

**Additional context**

Multiple Errors like this one:

```bash
error[E0432]: unresolved import `crate::Vec3`
 --> /Users/.../.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/glam-0.30.9/src/euler.rs:3:66
  |
3 | ...Vec3, Mat3, Mat3A, Mat4, Quat, Vec3, Vec3A, Vec3Swizzles};
  |                                   ^^^^
  |                                   |
  |                                   no `Vec3` in the root
  |                                   help: a similar name exists in the module (notice the capitalization): `vec3`
  |
  = help: consider importing this struct instead:
          crate::deref::Vec3
```

_Don't know if I'm missing something obvious._

title: Add dual quaternions
author: cactusdualcore
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

Dual quaternions can represent rigid body transformations and allow efficient on them.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

A `DualQuat` type added to this crate.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

Rolling my own from two `Quat`s.

**Additional context**

Dual quaternions can be very useful when working with physics simulations on rigid bodies.

title: Incorrect (overzealous) error checking in `new_inclusive` for vec types
author: nfagerlund

title: add arbitrary trait implementations
author: ScanMountGoat
body:
**What problem does this solve or what need does it fill?**

This makes it possible to derive `arbitrary::Arbitrary` for types using glam types.

**Describe the solution you'd like**

I'd like to have the trait implementation in glam as an optional feature similar to serde, encase, etc.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**

I currently use a derive attribute to specify a custom function for each field that uses a glam type. This works but can be a bit messy for some structs.

```rust
#[cfg_attr(feature = "serde", derive(Serialize, Deserialize))]
#[cfg_attr(feature = "arbitrary", derive(arbitrary::Arbitrary))]
#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Clone, Copy, Default)]
pub struct Transform {
    #[cfg_attr(feature = "arbitrary", arbitrary(with = arbitrary_vec3))]
    pub scale: Vec3,

    #[cfg_attr(feature = "arbitrary", arbitrary(with = arbitrary_quat))]
    pub rotation: Quat,

    #[cfg_attr(feature = "arbitrary", arbitrary(with = arbitrary_vec3))]
    pub translation: Vec3,
}
```

**Additional context**

The `arbitrary::Arbitrary` trait enables structured fuzzing with cargo-fuzz. I use fuzzing for testing the stability of conversion code for file formats for 3D models.

This shouldn't be too hard to implement. I would be happy to make a PR if this looks like a good addition for glam.

title: Patch version bump from 0.30.5 to 0.30.6 Caused a breaking change
author: fredizzimo
body:
This change https://github.com/bitshifter/glam-rs/pull/647 of changing the arguments from `self` to `&self` is a breaking change in combination with [Glamour](https://github.com/simonask/glamour) and causes errors like this
```rust
error[E0308]: mismatched types
   --> /home/fredizzimo/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/glamour-0.18.0/src/forward.rs:159:30
    |
159 |             <Self>::$fn_name(self $(, $arg_name)*)
    |             ---------------- ^^^^ expected `Vec2`, found `&Vec2`
    |             |
    |             arguments to this function are incorrect
    |
   ::: /home/fredizzimo/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/glamour-0.18.0/src/bindings/vec.rs:186:1
    |
186 | impl_vector2!(glam::Vec2, glam::Vec3);
    | ------------------------------------- in this macro invocation
    |
note: method defined here
   --> /home/fredizzimo/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/glam-0.30.6/src/f32/vec2.rs:129:18
    |
129 |     pub const fn to_array(self) -> [f32; 2] {
    |                  ^^^^^^^^
    = note: this error originates in the macro `crate::forward_fn_self_ref` which comes from the expansion of the macr
o `impl_vector2` (in Nightly builds, run with -Z macro-backtrace for more info)
help: consider dereferencing the borrow
    |
159 |             <Self>::$fn_name(*self $(, $arg_name)*)
```

So, I think it should have been released as 0.31.0 instead.

title: Rustdoc ICE triggered by glam
author: mintlu8
body:
**Describe the bug**

Glam triggers an internal compiler error on `docs.rs` with its SIMD code.

**To Reproduce**

Rustdoc build log:
https://docs.rs/crate/bevy_rich_text3d/0.4.1/builds/2366087

Does not happen locally with `cargo doc --open`
